entries.definition: It is a performance metric used in classification tasks to evaluate machine learning models by determining the proportion of correctly classified data objects.

It is a function that mathematically transforms the result of the input signal aggregation function into the output of an artificial neuron and enables an artificial neural network to model complex nonlinear relationships.
It is a type of hierarchical clustering that uses a bottom-up approach to build a cluster hierarchy, that is, by iteratively merging smaller clusters into larger ones.
It is a type of relationship used in frames that relates frames that describe parts of an object to frames that describe whole objects.

It is a type of path-cutting in the Alpha-beta pruning where the search is stopped below any node of the minimizer level whose beta value is less than or equal to the alpha value (beta≤alpha) of any of the node's direct ancestors.
It is a numerical value assigned by the Alpha-beta pruning to a node that is at the maximizer level of the game tree. It defines the best move that can be made to maximize the outcome of the game.
It is an optimized version of the Minimax algorithm that avoids evaluating all nodes in the game tree, thus improving the efficiency of searching the game tree.
It is an element of the game tree structure that represents a move allowed in the game.

It is an element of the state space graph structure that represents a transition between states or, in other words, an executable action in the problem-solving process. In order to make it clear from which state of the problem-solving process a transition occurs to which state, arcs in a state space graph must be directed.

entries.term: Area under the ROC Curve, AUC
entries.definition: It is a single numerical value that measures how well a machine learning classification model can distinguish between data objects with positive and negative class labels, with higher values indicating better model performance.

It is a term introduced by John McCarthy, Stanford Professor, in 1955. It applies both to the field of science and engineering and to computer systems that are developed in that field.Artificial intelligence, as a field of science and engineering, is a sub-field of computer science that deals with the research and development of systems that are capable of demonstrating behavior associated with the existence of intelligence.An artificial intelligence system is "a machine-based system that is designed to operate with varying levels of autonomy and that may exhibit adaptiveness after deployment, and that, for explicit or implicit objectives, infers, from the input it receives, how to generate outputs such as predictions, content, recommendations, or decisions that can influence physical or virtual environments" (EU Artificial Intelligence Act, 2024).
It is a computational system composed of interconnected artificial neurons that work together to process input data and generate outputs.
It is a basic structural unit in an artificial neural network that processes input signals to produce an output signal.

It is an artificial intelligence approach that involves the development of intelligent computer systems that surpass human capabilities (Russell and Norvig, 2020) and are, therefore, smarter than any human mind in any field, including scientific creativity, general wisdom and social skills (Bostrom, 2015).
It is a type of relationship used in frames that allows defining relations other than generalization and aggregation, based on the general referencing principle.
It is a type of unsupervised machine learning task in which combinations of data object feature values that frequently appear in the dataset are identified.

It is a type of linkage method used in hierarchical clustering in which, when determining cluster similarity, the distance between two clusters is defined as the average distance between each data object in one cluster to every data object in the other cluster.

It is a method for training artificial neural networks by calculating an error at the output of the network and propagating it backward through the network to update the weights of neuron connections.

It is a state space graph search control strategy used to explore possible paths in the state space graph, returning to unexplored nodes if a dead-end node is reached in a search process that is not the search goal node.

It is a search strategy used in state space graph search or production systems. It starts with known facts about the problem or initial state and iteratively applies applicable rules or operators to obtain new facts or states until the goal is reached.
It is a heuristic search algorithm that, when exploring a state space graph, examines and expands a limited number of nodes with the best heuristic measures at each iteration.
It is a predefined number that determines how many nodes with the best heuristic measures will be examined in each iteration of the Beam search algorithm.

It is a heuristic search algorithm that, when exploring a state space graph, prioritizes nodes with the best heuristic measures, thus efficiently moving towards the search goal node by switching between different paths in the state space graph.

It is a type of path-cutting in the Alpha-beta pruning where the search is stopped below any node of the maximizer level whose alpha value is higher than or equal to the beta value (beta≤alpha) of any of the node's direct ancestors.
It is a numerical value assigned by the Alpha-beta pruning to a node that is at the minimizer level of the game tree. It defines the best move that can be made to minimize the outcome of the game.

It is a real number that allows a machine learning model to shift a line or hyperplane away from the origin. Without this bias coefficient, the line or hyperplane would be forced to pass through the origin, resulting in a poor fit to the data in the dataset.

It is an uninformed search algorithm, a modification of Breadth-First search, that sequentially expands nodes at one level before moving to the next level, performing a search in a state space graph in both search directions simultaneously - data-driven search and goal-driven search - until it finds a point of contact in both directions.
It is a type of classification task in which data are divided into only two classes, and the output variable can take one of two possible values.

It is the average number of descendants of nodes in a state space graph, calculated by dividing the total number of direct descendants of all nodes in the state space graph by the total number of nodes in the graph.
It is an uninformed search algorithm that, when exploring a state space graph, sequentially expands nodes at one level before moving to the next level.
It is a data object in the center of a cluster around which data objects are grouped based on their distance to the centroid.

It was described by John Searle. It criticizes the physical symbol system hypothesis by showing that a physical symbol system cannot reach the human-level intelligence by mechanically manipulating symbols according to the defined rules. The experiment imagines a person in a room who does not know Chinese, but is able to respond correctly to signs written in Chinese using instructions that define how to interpret the signs. Searle argued that in this way the system does not gain understanding or intelligence despite the correct answers that are given.
It is a type of frames that represents common properties of a set/class/kind/type of objects/concepts.
It is a type of supervised machine learning task in which a machine learning model learns to assign new data objects to one of several predefined categories or classes.
It is the difference between the true class label given in the dataset for a certain data object and the class label predicted by a machine learning model.
It is a data structure used in the implementation of search algorithms that contains closed nodes or, in other words, nodes that have already been expanded in the state space graph search.
It is a node of a state space graph that has already been expanded into the state space graph by a search algorithm during the search process.
It is a grouping of data objects characterized by two aspects: a) similarity of data objects within a cluster and b) difference of data objects between clusters.
It is a type of unsupervised machine learning task in which data are segmented into representative classes called clusters.

It is a type of linkage method used in hierarchical clustering in which, when determining cluster similarity, the distance between the most distant data objects of the clusters (the longest distance) is used.

entries.term: Complexity of a state space graph
entries.definition: It is an estimate of how many nodes a search algorithm may have to visit in the worst case in a state space graph to find a solution.
It is a selection of one production rule from the conflict set of a production system.
It is a specific method used to resolve conflict in production systems.
It is a collection of all production rules currently matching the facts in the working memory of a production system.
It is a table that shows how often a machine learning classification model correctly or incorrectly classified data objects from specific classes.

It is one of the thinking paradigms in the field of artificial intelligence, in which intelligent behaviour is modelled by focusing on distributed representations, learning from data and pattern recognition through artificial neural networks without relying on explicitly defined rules and symbolic structures.
It is a component of a production system that performs inference by completing a pattern-directed search in the knowledge base.
It is a mathematical function that quantitatively evaluates the performance of a machine learning model and is used to optimize the model’s parameters.

It is a procedure (an autonomous code fragment) in a frame system that is automatically executed when performing operations with a slot value, such as reading or changing it. Such procedures provide access control to slot values stored in the frame system and help maintain the integrity of the entire system.

It is a particular unit of data typically represented by a number of feature values, for example, a data object can represent one specific patient through the values of patient’s temperature, blood pressure, weight, blood test results, etc.
It is a state space graph traversal strategy, where a search starts at a node from the root node set and moves forward (in the direction of the arcs) in the state space graph.
It is a set of data objects used by machine learning algorithms, for example, a set of data about patients who attended Hospital A between January and May in 2019.
It is a simple learning rule for artificial neural networks that updates perceptron’s weights proportionally to the difference between its predicted and known output.
It is an output of hierarchical clustering. It is a tree-like diagram representing hierarchical relationships between data objects in the dataset.
It is a predefined maximum depth that determines how deep a state space graph will be searched when performing a Depth-Limited Search.

entries.term: Depth of a state space graph
entries.definition: It is the length of the longest path from the root node to the deepest leaf node of a state space graph.
It is an uninformed search algorithm that explores a state space graph, always descending as deep as possible into it, searching for the search goal node.

It is an uninformed search algorithm, a modification of Depth-First Search, that explores a state space graph, always descending to a given depth limit in the state space graph while searching for the search goal node.

It is a type of unsupervised machine learning task which is associated with finding the most essential features in a dataset to reduce the original feature set, especially by dropping less important features or merging correlated features (Geron, 2017; Pant, 2019).
It is a type of hierarchical clustering that uses a top-down approach to build a cluster hierarchy, that is, by iteratively splitting larger clusters into smaller ones.
It is an individual with deep, specialized knowledge and experience in a particular domain, whose expertise is used in the development of knowledge-based intelligent computer systems.
It is one of the hyperparameters of an artificial neural network that determines how many times the entire training dataset is used during the training process.
It is a similarity metric used for continuous feature values that calculates the geometric distance between two data objects.

It is a knowledge-based intelligent computer system that solves problems in a narrowly specialised domain in a way and at a performance level similar to one of human experts (Luger, 2009; Negnevitsky, 2011).

It is a performance metric used in classification tasks to evaluate machine learning models by representing the harmonic mean of precision and recall, as a machine learning model achieves a high F1 score only when both recall and precision are high (Geron, 2017).

It is component of a frame that represents extended knowledge about slot values and tell the inference engine how to process the property of the object, allowing to set a slot value and controlling user's access to the slot value (Negnevitsky, 2011).
It is an outcome in classification tasks when the machine learning model predicts a negative class label for a data object, but the actual class label of the object is positive.
It is an outcome in classification tasks when the machine learning model predicts a positive class label for a data object, but the actual class label of the object is negative.
It is a measurable characteristic/property of a data object used as an input variable to a machine learning algorithm, for example, the patient’s temperature.

It is a set of all features characterising data objects in a dataset, for example, the patient's temperature, blood pressure, weight, blood test results, and others can represent one patient in the dataset.
It is a similarity metric that quantifies how alike two data objects are based on the values of their individual features.
It is a specific value of a data object feature, for example, if the feature is “the patient’s temperature”, then its value for a specific patient can be 38°C.

It is a type of artificial neural network in which neuron connections go in only one direction; that is, each neuron receives input signals from neurons in the previous layer and sends its output to neurons in the next layer.

It is a representation of an object, system or process in a precise and unambiguous way using a well-defined set of symbols, rules and structures that allow the resulting representation to be easily transformed into computational objects.

It is a search strategy used in state space graph search or production systems. It starts with known facts about the problem or initial state and iteratively applies applicable rules or operators to obtain new facts or states until the goal is reached.
It is a knowledge representation structure used in a frame-based expert system that represents typical information about an object/concept, its components and properties.
It is a knowledge-based intelligent computer system in which domain knowledge is represented in the form of frames.
It is a game tree that includes all states, from the initial state of the game (represented by the root node of the tree), to all goal states of the game (represented by leaf nodes).

It is a complete representation of the problem-solving process, including all possible states and transitions between them, showing all possible paths from the initial state to all possible goal states.

It is a heuristic measure, which in the case of a fully available game tree, is assigned to the root node, indicating what the outcome of the game will be if both players (human and computer) play rationally, leading the game to their victory and avoiding mistakes.
It is a type of a state space graph that formally represents the course of a game.

It is a type of relationship used in frames that defines a relationship between subclass and class frames, instance and subclass frames, and instance and class frames, allowing the creation of hierarchical levels where the most general frame is represented at the highest level of the hierarchy and specific instance frames at the lowest levels.
It is an end state of the problem-solving process, when all constraints are satisfied and the problem is solved.
It is a state space graph traversal approach, where a search starts with a node from the leaf node set and moves backward in the state space graph (against the direction of the arrows).

It is an optimization algorithm that iteratively adjusts parameters of a machine learning model in the direction of the steepest decrease of a cost function to minimize errors during machine learning model training.
It is a similarity metric used for categorical feature values. It measures the distance between two data objects as the number of features with differing values.

It is a mathematical function that assigns numerical values to nodes in a state space graph, representing their proximity or cost to reach the search goal and helping a search algorithm choose those paths in the state space graph that most likely lead to the search goal.
These are experience-based rules and methods that allow people to solve problems, make decisions, and reason more effectively, reducing cognitive load and the need for in-depth problem analysis.

entries.term: Heuristic knowledge (in state space graph search)
entries.definition: These are mathematically represented rules that allow a search algorithm to choose those paths in a state space graph that are most likely to lead to an acceptable problem solution, thus reducing the number of visited nodes and search time.

It is a numerical value assigned to a state space graph node by a heuristic evaluation function, expressing the quality of then ode, or the approximate cost of a path from that node to the search goal node.

It is a state space graph traversal strategy that uses additional information about the quality of nodes in a state space graph (i.e. whether a particular node allows and how quickly it allows reaching the search goal node) to select the most promising paths in the state space graph, reducing the number of explored nodes and improving performance.
It is a type of layer in an artificial neural network that is located between the input and output layers and contains neurons that perform numerical computations.
It is an unsupervised machine learning algorithm that builds a hierarchy of clusters.
It is a heuristic search algorithm that, when exploring a state space graph, selects and expands at each iteration one node having the best heuristic measure.

It is an effect of a depth limit to which the state space graph is generated or explored during a search process, and thus a search algorithm is unable to proceed beyond the specified depth limit, where the search goal node may still be located.

It is a threshold drawn across a dendrogram of hierarchical clustering to determine the number of clusters by “cutting” the hierarchy at a specific distance level, grouping all branches below the line into clusters.

It is an external configuration variable whose value is typically set by the developer arbitrarily before the training of a machine learning model (for example, learning rate, number of neurons in an artificial neural network, etc.).

It is a component of a knowledge-based intelligent computer system that interprets and evaluates facts stored in the knowledge base with the aim of supporting problem-solving, answering user’s questions or building an action plan (Ertel, 2011).
It is a process by which the local information of a class/set/type/kind node is inherited by a subclass/subset/subtype/subkind node or an instance node in semantic networks or frames.
It is a state that includes all known information, constraints, and available resources at the beginning of the problem-solving process and serves as a reference point for finding a solution.
It is a type of layer in an artificial neural network that receives input signals from the environment and passes them to the hidden layers without any processing.
It is a mathematical function that combines input signals of an artificial neuron. It is usually a summation function that calculates the weighted sum of the input signals.

They are numerical values that enter an artificial neuron from the environment or from other neurons. Numerical values from the environment usually correspond to the feature values of a data object in a dataset. An artificial neuron can have several inputs.
It is a type of frames that represents detailed knowledge about a particular object/concept in a problem domain.

These are cognitive abilities and behaviour, usually attributed to animals such as monkeys, dolphins, elephants, rats, crows, etc., and linked to the ability to perceive, interpret and respond adaptively to environmental stimuli.

It is the result of neural activity in the brain, determining the human capacity to acquire, understand, and apply knowledge and skills through processes such as reasoning, problem-solving, memory, learning, and adaptation to new situations. It can be conceptualized from two perspectives: as a single ability (general intelligence) or a set of abilities (like linguistic, spatial, emotional and other types of intelligence).

It is attributed to manifestations of intellectual behavior such as the ability to learn based on repeated practice, remember, perceive multiple aspects of the environment, sense location, etc., which are not based on the existence of a brain and nervous system.

It is a software and/or hardware system that uses artificial intelligence methods — such as machine learning, natural language processing, computer vision, and others — to perform tasks that typically require intelligence, including problem-solving, learning, reasoning, perception, decision-making, and adapting to dynamic environments.

It is a type of node in a state space graph that corresponds to an intermediate state of the problem-solving process. In terms of the structure of a state space graph, it is a node that has both ancestors and descendants.

It is a technique for implementing deterministic two-player games with perfect information and time constraints, which can be based on the principles of both the Minimax algorithm and Alpha-beta pruning. It evaluates nodes of a game sub-tree gradually, level by level, keeping track of the time remaining to complete a move.

It is an unsupervised machine learning algorithm that attributes data objects to a predefined number of clusters, grouping similar data objects in such a way that the distance between the data objects belonging to the cluster and the cluster centroid is minimized, while achieving the greatest possible difference between different clusters.

It is a supervised machine learning algorithm used for both classification and regression tasks, where a class or numerical value of a new data object is determined based on the feature frequencies of a preselected number of the most similar data objects in the dataset.
It is an automated system that could be used either by the domain expert directly or by the knowledge engineer to describe knowledge and to build a knowledge base.
It is a component of a knowledge-based intelligent computer system that stores domain knowledge in the form of computational objects and relationships.
It is a specialist who designs and maintains a knowledge base in a knowledge-based intelligent computer system.
It addresses the problem of capturing the full range of knowledge required for intelligent behaviour in a formal language (Graham and Barrett, 1997).
It is a set of formal conventions and rules used to describe knowledge.

It is a computer system that stores a formally represented body of domain-specific knowledge and employs reasoning algorithms to process that knowledge to solve problems, make decisions, or generate explanations.
It is a component of the hierarchical structure of an artificial neural network, consisting of a group of artificial neurons that perform computations and pass their outputs to the next layer.

entries.term: Leaf node (in a state space graph)
entries.definition: It is a type of node of a state space graph that corresponds to the goal state of the problem-solving process. In terms of the structure of a state space graph, it is a node that has no descendants.

It is a hyperparameter that determines the size of the steps a machine learning algorithm takes when updating model parameters during training; for example, in linear regression, it reflects a speed at which weights of the linear function are updated, and in artificial neural networks, it reflects a speed at which weights between neuron connections are updated.
It is a supervised machine learning algorithm that predicts a continuous numerical value based on an identified linear relationship between input data (input variables) and a single output variable.
It is a hyperparameter in hierarchical clustering that defines how the distance between clusters is calculated when merging or splitting clusters.

It is a drawback of the Hill Climbing algorithm when the algorithm does not reach the search goal node, because during the search it finds a node whose heuristic measure is better than the heuristic measures of its descendants/ancestors (depending on a search direction).

They are schemes that represent knowledge in the form of logical statements, using constants, variables, functions, predicates, logical operators, and apply inference rules that allow getting new true knowledge from a set of true knowledge.
It is a supervised machine learning algorithm used for binary classification tasks that predicts a categorical outcome by learning a linear relationship between input variables and the output class.

It is a sub-field of artificial intelligence that develops methods and algorithms for automatically discovering mathematical relationships in data to create systems that can analyze data and improve their performance in performing tasks without being specifically programmed for those tasks.

It is a computational method that allows a system to automatically learn mathematical patterns in data and base its future actions on these relationships, rather than following explicitly programmed step-by-step instructions like classical programming algorithms.
It is a similarity metric used for continuous feature values that calculates the distance between two data objects as the sum of the absolute differences of their feature values.

It is a conventional designation for one of the players in a computer implementation of deterministic two-person games with perfect information. This player's strategy is to make moves that will lead him to the win of the game.

It is a performance metric used in regression tasks to evaluate machine learning models. It calculates the average of the absolute differences between the true labels of data objects and the predicted output values.

It is a performance metric used in regression tasks to evaluate machine learning models. It calculates the average of the squared prediction errors for individual data objects. It is a positive value that is not equal to zero.

It is an algorithm used to develop deterministic two-person games with perfect information that evaluates all nodes in a game tree, looking ahead at possible moves and choosing those that lead to the best outcome for a given player, while taking into account the opponent's best moves.

It is a conventional designation for one of the players in a computer implementation of deterministic two-person games with perfect information. This player's strategy is to make moves that prevent the other player, called the maximizer, from winning the game.
It is a mathematically expressed relationship (mapping) between input and output data of a machine learning algorithm, characterized by certain configuration variables called parameters.
It is a result of applying a machine learning model to given data objects.
It is a type of classification task in which data can be assigned to more than two classes, and the output variable can take one of several possible values.
It is an artificial neural network composed of multiple layers of perceptrons, including one or more hidden layers, that can model complex nonlinear relationships between inputs and outputs.
It is a type of linear regression that predicts a continuous numerical value based on a linear relationship between several input variables and one output variable.

It is a technique for the implementation of deterministic two-person games with perfect information that can be based on both the Minimax algorithm and Alpha-beta pruning. It processes a sub-tree of a game tree generated to a given depth and uses a heuristic evaluation function to evaluate the quality of nodes and select the next move.
It is a class label used in a machine learning classification task that represents the absence of an condition or state of interest (often represented by 0).
They are schemas that represent knowledge in the form of a directed labelled graph, where each node displays a simple object/concept and directed labelled arcs represent relations between them.

It is an artificial intelligence approach that focuses on studying a broader set of organisms, processes and phenomena at different spatial and temporal scales, viewing intelligence as a capability that arises when agents with simpler behaviours interact in real environments and together achieve more complex behaviours.
It is an element of the game tree structure corresponding to a discrete state of the game.
It is an element of the state space graph structure corresponding to a discrete state of the problem-solving process.
It is a process of finding all direct descendants/ancestors (depending on a search direction) of the node under consideration in the state space graph search, looking for a solution to the problem.

It is a characteristic of a game tree for a zero-sum deterministic two-person game with perfect information. At the node level, one or more nodes representing game states are arranged, from which only one of the two players makes a move.
It is a type of frames in which daemons are attached to frame slots.

It is a data structure used in the implementation of search algorithms that contains open nodes or, in other words, nodes that are generated in the state space graph search, but whose descendants/ancestors (depending on the search direction) have not yet been expanded.
It is a node of a state space graph that a search algorithm has already found in the state space graph during the search process, but has not yet expanded it.

It is a numerical value obtained by processing the given input signals in an artificial neuron, most often by applying an input signal aggregation function and an activation function. An artificial neuron has a single output.
It is a type of layer in an artificial neural network that contains neurons performing numerical processing of input signals to produce the network’s output and transmit it to the environment.
It is an internal configuration variable of a machine learning model whose value is found (learnt) by a machine learning algorithm during the training.

entries.term: Parameters characterizing states in the game
entries.definition: These are all the information or factors that change during the course of a game after a move has been made and determine an exact state of the game at that moment.
It is a sub-tree of the full game tree that contains states generated from the current state to a specified depth.

It is a subgraph of a state space graph that is typically dynamically generated during the operation of an intelligent computer system up to a specified depth (not up to all leaf nodes) due to limited computational resources.
It is a description of the current state of problem-solving process in production systems.

It is a method used by a production system’s inference mechanism to systematically match production rules in the knowledge base with current facts in the working memory and execute the rules whose conditions are satisfied to advance problem-solving or reasoning.
It is a basic type of artificial neuron that computes a weighted sum of its inputs, applies an activation function, and produces a binary output.

It comprises symbols representing domain knowledge, experience, concepts and causal relationships, symbolic structures made of symbols connected in a particular way, and operations to create and use the symbolic structures.

It is a proposition in the philosophy of artificial intelligence, formulated by Allen Newell and Herbert A. Simon in 1976: "A physical symbol system has the necessary and sufficient means for general intelligent action".

It is a drawback of the Hill Climbing algorithm, where the algorithm is unable to continue traversing the state space graph if the heuristic measure of the best descendant/ancestor of the node under consideration is equal to the heuristic measure of the node itself.

It is a class label used in a machine learning classification task that represents an event or condition of interest that a machine learning model is trying to detect or predict (often represented by 1).

It is a performance metric used in classification tasks to evaluate machine learning models by determining the proportion of data objects predicted as positive whose actual class label is also positive.
It is the difference between the actual output value given in the dataset for a certain data object and the value predicted by a machine learning model.
These are schemes that represent knowledge in the form of a set of procedures/instructions.
It is a unit of problem-solving knowledge, expressed as: IF condition THEN action/consequence.
It is a rule-based expert system in which the knowledge base is represented as a set of production rules.

It is a linear data structure that follows the First In, First Out (FIFO) principle, meaning the first data element added is the first one to be processed. It is used to implement the OPEN list in Breadth-First Search.

It is a performance metric used in classification tasks to evaluate machine learning models by calculating the proportion of data objects predicted as positive compared to the total number of data objects in the dataset with positive class labels.

It is a graph that shows how well a machine learning classification model is able to distinguish between positive and negative class data objects at different classification decision thresholds, which determine at what prediction value a data object is assigned to the positive class.
It is a type of artificial neural network in which the outputs of the neurons are fed back into their own inputs.
It is a mechanism that allows frames to be linked together by using the title of one frame as a slot value in another frame.

It is a type of supervised machine learning task in which a machine learning model learns to predict a continuous numerical value (for example, house price, number of graduates, etc.) for new data objects.

It is a reward-based machine learning suitable for agents (such as robots or game software) that operate in a dynamic environment and need to find an optimal sequence of actions to maximize the achievement of an ultimate goal.

It is a performance metric used in regression tasks to evaluate machine learning models by applying the square root to the mean squared error, thus providing the error in the same  measurement units as the target variable.

entries.term: Root node (in a state space graph)
entries.definition: It is a type of node of a state space graph that corresponds to an initial state of the problem-solving process. In terms of the structure of a state space graph, it is a node that has no ancestors.
It is a knowledge-based intelligent computer system in which domain knowledge is represented in the form of a set of rules (Hopgood, 2001).
It is a performance metric used in regression tasks that indicates how well regression model’s predictions match labels of data objects in a dataset.

It is a method for finding a solution to a problem in a state space graph representing the problem by systematically processing nodes of the graph based on a known search start node and a search goal node.
It is a technique used in a production system to make searching for production rules in a knowledge base more efficient.
It is an approach to node exploration used in state space graph search to find a solution to a problem.
It is a specific node in a state space graph that the search process wants to reach, as it indicates the finding of a solution to the problem.
It is a specific node in a state space graph from in which the search for a solution to the problem is started using a search algorithm.

It is a network-based knowledge representation schema that represents knowledge in the form of a graph, whose labeled nodes represent objects/concepts, while labeled and directed arcs represent relationships between these objects/concepts.
It is a visual representation used to assess the quality of clustering. It shows how well each data object fits into its cluster and how different the clusters are from each other.

It is a metric used to evaluate clustering quality. It measures how well each data object fits within its cluster compared to other clusters, with higher values indicating clearer and better-separated clusters.
It is a function that quantifies the similarity between two data objects to support such tasks of machine learning as classification, clustering, and recommendation.

It is a type of linkage method used in hierarchical clustering in which, when determining cluster similarity, the distance between the closest data objects of the clusters (the shortest distance) is used.
It is the simplest type of an artificial neural network composed of a single layer of perceptrons that directly maps inputs to outputs and can only process linearly separable functions.
It is a title of a property of an object/concept represented in the frame.

It is a path in a state space graph that starts at the given search start node, ends at the given search goal node, and corresponds to the ordered sequence of actions that must be performed to solve the problem.

It is a linear data structure that follows the Last In, First Out (LIFO) principle, meaning the last data element added is the first one to be processed. It is used to implement the OPEN list in Depth-First Search.
It is the set of values of dynamic properties characterising the behaviour and structure of a given object, system or phenomenon at a given point in time.

It is a formal representation of the problem-solving process, where the states of the problem-solving process are represented as nodes and the transitions between states or, in other words, actions to be taken in the problem-solving process as arcs.

entries.term: Strong artificial intelligence (also artificial general intelligence)
entries.definition: It is also known as human-level intelligence (Russell and Norvig, 2020). It is an artificial intelligence approach that envisions the development of computer systems capable of solving a wide range of tasks across different problem domains and autonomously controlling themselves with their own thoughts, concerns, emotions, strengths, weaknesses, and tendencies (Pennachin and Goertzel, 2007).
They are based on the use of broad and in-depth knowledge about a specific problem, taking into account problem-solving context.
These are schemas that use a graph to represent knowledge, where each node is a complex structure containing the properties and components that characterize an object or concept.

It is a type of machine learning, also called learning with a teacher, where a machine learning model is trained on data containing labels of the desired solution or answer to learn a mapping describing a relationship between input data and output label.

It is a branch of the new of artificial intelligence, based on research into the behaviour of social animals such as ants, bees and birds. This branch believes that collective intelligence arises from the interaction and cooperation of many less intelligent agents working together to solve complex problems using a decentralised and self-organising approach.

It is one of the thinking paradigms in the field of artificial intelligence, in which intelligent behaviour is modelled using explicit symbolic representations of problems and their solutions and rules for manipulating these symbols. This paradigm is based on the physical symbol system hypothesis.
It is a subset of the dataset that is used in the testing of a machine learning model.
It is a process of checking the performance of a machine learning model, usually using data previously unseen by the machine learning algorithm.

It is an extension of the Turing Test, which tests not only the ability of an intelligent computer system to communicate in natural language, but also its ability to interact with the physical world by perceiving and moving objects.
It is a process of determining the best values of parameters comprising a machine learning model and identifying relationships and patterns in the data.
It is a subset of the dataset that is used in the training of a machine learning model.
It is an outcome in classification tasks when the machine learning model’s prediction of a negative class label matches the actual negative class label of the data object.
It is an outcome in classification tasks when the machine learning model’s prediction of a positive class label matches the actual positive class label of the data object.

It is one of the fundamental tests of artificial intelligence, proposed by Alan Turing in 1950. It tests the ability of an intelligent computer system to produce answers to textual questions that are indistinguishable from human answers.
It is a state space graph search strategy that sequentially and systematically explores paths in a state space graph without any additional information about the location of the search goal node.
It is a type of linear regression that predicts a continuous numerical value based on a linear relationship between a single input variable and a single output variable.

It is a type of machine learning, also called learning without a teacher, where a model is trained on data without labels of the desired solution or answer to identify mathematical patterns, structures, or relationships within the input data.

entries.term: Weak artificial intelligence (also artificial narrow intelligence)
entries.definition: It is an approach within artificial intelligence focused on creating intelligent computer systems designed to perform specific tasks or solve particular problems, but that do not have the ability to learn and perform tasks outside of the predefined domain.

They use general approaches such as generic search, logic, syntax processing, automated reasoning and other computations that can be applied to a wide range of problems without deep knowledge of the problem at hand.

They are numerical values that represent the strength of an artificial neuron's input, or in other words, the importance of each neuron input, taking into account its influence on the artificial neuron's output. Each input of an artificial neuron has its weight.

It is a path that starts at the root node of a game tree and goes to the dead end node, satisfying the following conditions: all nodes of the path are interconnected and heuristic measures of all nodes included in the path are equal to the game result.

It is a component of a production system that stores a current state of problem solving, which is characterized by facts obtained from the user, conclusions drawn during the inference process, information sent by external programs, etc.

entries.term: Zero-sum deterministic two-person game with perfect information
entries.definition: It is a competitive game in which two players (human-human or human-computer) take turns alternatively, knowing at every moment of the game exactly what situation has arisen and what can be done next, and one player's gain is exactly equal to the other player's loss.
